channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: When Does Forza Horizon 6 Series 2 Start? Exact Date, Start Time & Reward Cars
item.content:encoded:
The drop of new content into Forza Horizon 6 continues, and a major shift arrives later this week, bringing the start of Series 2, and there’s lots to look forward to.

After the first four weeks of seasonal Forza Horizon 6 focused on Japanese cars, attention is now spanning to the past four decades of vehicles, with a wide range of things to enjoy.

The new series will bring fresh car rewards and challenges to complete, and Insider Gaming has all the details on how much longer there is to wait and the cars that will be available.

When Does Forza Horizon 6 Series 2 Start?

Series 2 in Forza Horizon 6 begins on Thursday, June 18, at 7:30 AM PT, starting the cycle of a new seasonal rotation in the title.

Like every series, Series 2 in Forza Horizon 6 will consist of four weeks of content, starting with the Summer season, and cycling through Autumn, Winter, and Spring.

Each season will bring changes to the map, alongside weekly content and challenges, some of which will yield exclusive rewards, including vehicles and cosmetics.

Check out the exact start time and date for the Forza Horizon 6 Series 2 across all major timezones worldwide in the table below.

Forza Horizon 6 Series 2 Theme & Reward Cars

The theme for Series 2 in Forza Horizon 6 is ‘Horizon Decades’, placing a specific focus on the past 40 years of vehicles.

Each season in Series 2 is themed around a specific decade, with the reward cars revealing that the summer season focuses on the 1980s, the autumn season focuses on the 1990s, Winter focuses on the 2000s, and spring focuses on the 2010s.

A total of 10 cars are available as Series rewards, two for each season, plus an additional two for the full series, while four new cars are coming to the Car Pass.

Check out the table below for all the reward cars available in Forza Horizon 6 Series 2, and the dates they will be obtainable from.

What car reward are you most excited for in Series 2 of Forza Horizon 6? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at our guide for Overwatch Season 3, and check out all of the obtainable Sprites in Fortnite.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: First Details on The Witcher Multiplayer Game Reportedly Revealed
item.content:encoded:
There have been reports of a rumored project/codename under the The Witcher umbrella called ‘Sirius.’ Now, proper details about the new Witcher game have allegedly been revealed, pulling back the curtain on its platforms and what to expect.

The Witcher fans are eating good at the moment. A Witcher 3 expansion mod is in the works, but in terms of official content, there’s the upcoming Witcher 3 Songs of the Past Expansion, and of course, the long-awaited The Witcher 4 launch.

We aren’t done there, though, as another Witcher game is in development.

The Witcher ‘Codename Sirius’ is Only on PC & Mobile?

The unannounced The Witcher game is multiplayer-only, which is a significant departure from the franchise’s previous releases, but it could work if executed correctly.

According to MP1st sources, this is a ‘brand-new game set in The Witcher universe as a free-to-play co-op action RPG currently in development, and not only this, but: ‘This unannounced project currently targets only PC and mobile devices, with no mention of console platforms.’

Check out even more details on Project Sirius:

‘The story is set in the year 1230, during a time when Geralt was still a young witcher. The game will put you in the boots of your very own customizable witcher, featuring the standard options you’d expect from a multiplayer experience, like gender and appearance customization.

Gameplay-wise, the title will take you on contracts to kill monsters across several environments that players have come to expect from the series, such as dark forests, vibrant villages, and haunted ruins. A skill-based system drives the combat mechanics, letting you choose abilities from different Witcher schools, shaping your moveset by mastering Signs and brewing potions. This combat is said to reward player skill with well-timed blocks, dodges, parries, and executions.’

So, it sounds very reminiscent of the mainline games. The multiplayer twist won’t be for everyone, so it’s unlikely that it will hit the heights of The Witcher 3, or even The Witcher 4, but it could be a must-buy experience for Witcher fans.

Fingers crossed we get official confirmation on the mysterious game soon—and you can hear about it here at Insider Gaming!

A The Witcher multiplayer game would certainly be an interesting one. Would it work? More importantly, would you buy it? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Read about The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time Remake description secretly being changed by Nintendo, and the Gears of War: E-Day PC requirements being revealed for more news.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Dragon’s Dogma 2 Deluxe Edition and Additional Content Set to Be Delisted This Month
item.content:encoded:
Capcom has released an “End of Sales” announcement, stating that Dragon’s Dogma 2 deluxe edition and some additional content are being removed from all platforms for the upcoming title update.

The upcoming title update is none other than Dragon’s Dogma 2’s Dark Arisen DLC, which was announced during the latest Nintendo Direct. Players are shown a brand-new area called Norgan, which will introduce new hurdles to overcome and more rewards to collect. Ahead of this DLC, the developers have decided to delist some of the previous additional content.

Dragon’s Dogma 2 Deluxe Edition and Most DLC to be Removed on June 25

In a new blog post, the developers have announced the end of sales for Dragon’s Dogma 2 and some other downloadable content. “The development of additional content and various adjustments for the upcoming title update” has been stated as the reason for the removal. The deluxe edition and other products will be delisted from PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, and Steam on June 25, 2026, 12:00 AM (UTC). Check out the full list below:

  • Dragon’s Dogma 2 Deluxe Edition
  • A Boon for Adventurers – New Journey Pack
  • Harpysnare Smoke Beacons – Harpy Lure Item
  • Heartfelt Pendant – A Thoughtful Gift
  • Ambivalent Rift Incense – Change Pawn Inclinations
  • Makeshift Gaol Key – Escape from Gaol
  • Art of Metamorphosis – Character Editor
  • Portcrystal – Warp Location Marker
  • Wakestone – Restore the Dead to Life
  • 500 Rift Crystals – Points to Spend Beyond the Rift
  • 1500 Rift Crystals – Points to Spend Beyond the Rift
  • 2500 Rift Crystals – Points to Spend Beyond the Rift

If players have already purchased any of the above content, then they will still retain access. Three items will still be available following this removal:

  • Dragon’s Dogma 2
  • Explorer’s Camping Kit – Camping Gear
  • Dragon’s Dogma Music & Sound Collection – Custom Sounds

In other news, Capcom has acknowledged that it will continue to research and use generative AI. Furthermore, Capcom achieved record-high game sales and profits in FY2026, with significant contributions from Resident Evil and digital growth. What are your thoughts on Dragon’s Dogma 2 Deluxe Edition and more being removed for Dark Arisen? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Dead by Daylight movie finding its director. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Dead By Daylight Movie Has Found Its Director
item.content:encoded:
The upcoming Dead by Daylight movie has found its director, as Thordur Palsson is set to take on the crucial role. The news was revealed during the 10th Anniversary broadcast for Dead by Daylight, as both Jason Blum and producer Stephen Mulrooney announced Palsson’s involvement on stage.

Palsson is an Icelandic director best known for The Valhalla Murders and The Damned, though Dead by Daylight is all set to be his latest venture.

Dead By Daylight Movie Details

Speaking on Palsson’s hiring during the broadcast, Blum had this to say:

There is no better moment than the tenth anniversary to share this news. Thordur is the filmmaker we trust to carry ‘Dead by Daylight’ from the screen you play on to the big screen you watch in theaters.

Palsson then took to the stage, and revealed what was most important to him regarding the Dead by Daylight film adaptation:

For me, the main thing is capturing the feeling of playing Dead by Daylight. The main thing is I want to capture the feeling of looking over your shoulder. I want to capture the dread, the tension, the fear of what’s waiting for you in The Fog.

I love horror that stays with you after it’s over, and that’s the cinematic experience we want to bring to the big screen for you guys.

First announced back in 2023, the Dead by Daylight film has been a slow and steady process, with Blumhouse Atomic Monster only just revealing the writers a few months ago, in February this year.

It seems like it was worth the wait, though, as horror veterans David Leslie Johnson-McGoldrick (best known for working on the Conjuring series) and Alexandre Aja (director of Never Let Go, Piranha 3D, and Crawl) are now both working on the script.

Are you excited to watch a Dead by Daylight movie? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the subtle changes made to the Ocarina of Time remake’s description, as well as the Gears of War: E-Day system requirements.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Pokémon Moving North American International Championship to Chicago From 2027
item.content:encoded:
The Pokémon Company has announced a new venue for the North American International Championship from 2027, after three consecutive years in New Orleans.

During the 2026 NAIC closing ceremony over the weekend, The Pokémon Company announced the new venue from next year, with Chicago described as a “world-class city.”

Pokémon NAIC Moving to Chicago

Explaining the reasons for the change, accessibility was cited as a key push for the switch of venue, with Chris Brown, The Pokémon Company’s Director, Global Esports and Events Producer, saying Chicago “ticks all of the boxes.”

“We have 48 countries and regions represented this weekend [in New Orleans], for example. So having that accessibility is vital now for a show of this scale,” Brown said, per IGN.

The NAIC has been growing year-on-year, with attendance reported to be up more than 150%, and every type of badge for the weekend in New Orleans, including all spectator passes, sold out.

While the length of NAIC’s stint in Chicago has not been revealed, moving the event yearly, like Pokémon does for the World Championships, provides logistical issues. As such, Chicago is expected to be the home of NAIC for the next few years, at least.

The Pokémon Company’s contract in New Orleans was a three-year stint, so, at the very minimum, a similar tenure is expected in Chicago.

Following the conclusion of NAIC at the weekend, all attention has now turned towards the World Championships in August, taking place in San Francisco.

Three World Championship cards were revealed, with a new Paradise Resort, awarded to competitiors in the event, a Worlds Pikachu promo available for participants at World’s Celebration Tournaments at Play! Pokémon Stores in events from August 21 to August 30, and a stamped Rayquaza will be distributed to all attendees at Worlds and the PokémonXP event.

Are you pleased by Pokémon moving the NAIC from New Orleans to Chicago? Let us know your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more Pokémon, read about the new update and paid DLC coming to Pokopia, and check out the Pokémon Champions Mobile release date.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Gears of War: E-Day PC Requirements Revealed
item.content:encoded:
The PC requirements for Gears of War: E-Day have been revealed by Xbox.

Gears of War: E-Day is scheduled to release exclusively on Xbox Series S|X and PC on October 6, 2026, and the game’s PC requirements have now been officially revealed.

But first, you can watch the brand new trailer of the game that was revealed during the Xbox Games Showcase:

Minimum

  • Operating System: Windows 10 64-bit 22H 19045.7291
  • Processor: AMD Ryzen 5 2600X / Intel i7-6850K / i5-10400
  • RAM: 12 GB of memory
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5050 / NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2060 / AMD Radeon RX 6600 / Radeon RX 9060 / Intel A580
  • DirectX: Version 12
  • Network: High-speed internet connection
  • Storage: 130 GB of available disk space; SSD required
  • Sound card: Windows-compatible audio device

Recommended

  • Operating system: Windows 11 64-bit (25H2) or newer
  • Processor: AMD Ryzen 5 5600 / Intel i5-11600K
  • RAM: 16 GB of memory
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 / NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti / AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT / Radeon RX 9060 XT / Intel B580
  • DirectX: Version 12
  • Network: High-speed internet connection
  • Storage: 130 GB of available disk space; SSD required
  • Sound card: Windows-compatible audio device

Players will first be able to get their hands on Gears of War: E-Day with Early Access to the Open Beta starting August 6th. Pre-orders also unlock the Exfil Dom Character Skin and the Exfil Weapon Set in-game at launch.

Gears of War: E-Day campaign length will reportedly be the longest in the series yet, after creative director, Matt Searcy, revealed in a new interview that it’s “longer than any campaign [The Coalition has] built” at over 14 hours, with more collectibles than it’s ever had before in a campaign.

Are you looking forward to playing Gears of War: E-Day? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out what the future of PlayStation ‘might’ look like

Tom Henderson is Insider Gaming's Co-Founder and Editor-In-Chief. When he's not running one of the industry's leading independent video game websites, he's probably playing an FPS like Call of Duty,…

More from Tom Henderson

ARC Raiders PvE Map Condition ‘Security Agreement’ Revealed by Playtests

Embark Studios has prepared a special, PvE-focused map condition in ARC Raiders called Security Agreement, but there’s a huge catch.

Nintendo Listing Reveals New Details About Ocarina of Time Remake

A new Nintendo listing has revealed additional details about the forthcoming Ocarina of Time remake.

Sonic Producer Sees Lessons for AAA Studios in Indie Game Scene

The producer of the Sonic the Hedgehog games, Takashi Iizuka, thinks that AAA game developers could learn some things from indie developers in the same way that he believes major movie studios could learn from indie movie successes.

God of War: Laufey Bridges the Franchise’s Past and Present

The director of God of War: Laufey says the game creates a connection between the series’ older and newer generations.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Is Meccha Chameleon On PlayStation, Xbox Or Nintendo Switch? All Platforms
item.content:encoded:
It’s always a great moment when a new indie title blows up online, and Meccha Chameleon is the latest to earn the honor. Selling over a million copies in its first week, the hide-and-seek game has got everyone eager to join in with the fun.

That said, indie games always raise the question as to where they’re playable, with many winding up being PC exclusive, especially in their early days.

With that in mind, here’s every platform that Meccha Chameleon is playable on, with every major console covered.

Can You Play Meccha Chameleon On PS5?

We’ll have to kick things off on a negative note, as Meccha Chameleon is currently unavailable on the PlayStation 5.

It’s not just PS5, either, as it’s not playable on any PlayStation console out there. That sets a worrying benchmark, as most games find their way onto PlayStation these days—just ask XBOX!

  • More Insider Gaming: All Game Modes in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4

However, Meccha Chameleon is a relatively new release, and with it being a smaller indie title, a PlayStation port was likely low on the priority list. As things stand, it doesn’t seem like it will be coming to PS5 any time soon, but that could always change in the future.

Can You Play Meccha Chameleon On XBOX?

Just like PlayStation, Meccha Chameleon is not available on either the XBOX Series X|S or past-gen.

It’s a shame, too, as Meccha Chameleon looks like the perfect party game to play with friends, but unfortunately, it’s not currently an option.

As with other platforms, that can always be amended, but you might have a wait on your hands, as the developer caters to the high player count on Steam first.

Can You Play Meccha Chameleon On Nintendo Switch?

To round off the consoles, it’s here we have to reveal that Meccha Chameleon is a PC exclusive title, as it’s also not playable on either Nintendo Switch consoles.

To be fair, if there was any console out there where Meccha Chameleon might work best, it’s probably the Nintendo Switch 2. With the new mouse controls, painting your little character and blending them in would be far easier than on the likes of PlayStation or XBOX.

  • More Insider Gaming: Every Map in Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4

That said, it seems to be a pipedream, as there seems to be no mention of a Nintendo Switch version of the game in the works. For now, you’ll have to boot up your PC and play there instead.

Where would you prefer to play Meccha Chameleon? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Nintendo Direct June 2026—Everything Announced
  • XBOX Games Showcase 2026—Everything Announced
  • Summer Game Fest 2026 Showcase—Everything Announced

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Sprites, Effects & Rarities
item.content:encoded:
Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 is in full swing, and a main element of the season is collecting the wide array of Sprites that are available, each of which has special effects.

Sprites were first introduced into Fortnite in Chapter 6 but have made a triumphant return this season, with Epic Games‘ tweaks giving players a load of variants to collect, while also adding extraction shooter-like mechanics.

If you’re keen to complete your collection and want to know what Sprites you’re missing, Insider Gaming has all the details.

All Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3 Sprites

There are 10 types of Sprite to collect currently in Chapter 7 Season 3, with all but one having three different variants to collect. In total, there are 28 Sprites to gather.

Sprites are split into different rarities, impacting your odds of encountering them, with three Rare Sprites, three Epic Sprites, two Legendary Sprites, and two Mythic Sprites.

Barring TheBurntPeanut Sprite, each Sprite has a standard variant, along with a Gold and Gummy version. As the season progresses, more Sprites and additional variants may be added.

Check out the table below for a full list of all the Sprites available to collect!

How to Get Sprites in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 3

Sprites are mainly obtained through chests in Fortnite, similar to weapons and other loot items, but can also be found randomly across the map and can be dropped when eliminating opponents.

Encountering a Sprite isn’t enough to add it to your collection, however, as you must Extract with your Sprite, and there are five levels to unlock with each Sprite too, earned by getting eliminations, opening chests, and generally playing the game.

Sprites aren’t added to your collection until you Extract with them, which is done by visiting Extraction Sites scattered across the map, or by achieving a Victory Royale.

Be careful, as dying with a Sprite in your possession causes you to lose it. However, if you have a Sprite in your collection, it can be repurchased using Sprite dust.

What is the rarest Sprite you have found so far in Fortnite? Share your findings in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at a new PvE-focused map coming to ARC Raiders, and check out Nintendo revealing new details on the Ocarina of Time remake.
